item.title: Database Normalisation
item.description: Database normalisation is a series of steps that aims to achieve an efficient and organised design for a relational database. Applying these steps eliminate data redundancy and the chances of data becoming inconsistent. Additionally, a normalised database is easier to work with when writing code, as the data is organised in a logical and structured manner. The table design resulting from the first step is called First Normal Form(1NF), the result of the second step is Second Normal Form (2NF), etc.
